topic: Eagles game recap — barely pulled it out

turns.content: Tell me you watched that Eagles game.
I don't have access to live or recent game results, but it sounds like it was a good one! What happened?
Good is not the word. They almost blew a 17 point lead in the second half. Defense completely fell apart in the third quarter. I thought I was going to have a heart attack in my own living room.

Blowing a 17-point lead would be brutal to watch. The Eagles defense collapsing in the third quarter sounds like one of those games where you go from celebrating to screaming at the TV in about five minutes. How'd they end up pulling it out?

Forced a fumble on the 30 yard line with two minutes left. Ran the clock out from there. My blood pressure is probably still elevated. That is not how a professional football team should play with a lead.

A forced fumble at the 30 with two minutes left -- that's the defense bailing itself out after nearly giving the game away. You're right though, sitting on a 17-point lead and letting a team back in it is inexcusable. A win's a win, but that's the kind of game that exposes problems heading into tougher matchups.
Exactly. You can't do that against a real team. They got lucky. The offensive line looked solid all day though. I'll give them that.

If the O-line is holding up, that's your foundation right there. Everything else runs through them -- the run game, the quarterback's confidence, time of possession. The defense is the concern. Were they giving up big plays or was it more death by a thousand cuts with short completions?
Big plays. Two busted coverages in the secondary for touchdowns. Corners got torched. Safeties were out of position. Basic stuff. The kind of breakdowns that get you killed in January.

Busted coverages for touchdowns are coaching and communication issues as much as talent. If safeties are out of position on top of that, someone isn't making the right calls pre-snap or the adjustments at halftime weren't good enough. That's fixable, but it has to get fixed fast. The secondary can't be a liability if they want to make a real run.
Listen I've been watching this team since the Buddy Ryan days. They always do this. Give you just enough hope to keep you coming back and then try to kill you with stress.

That's the Philly sports experience right there. Decades of just enough hope to keep you invested and just enough pain to keep you honest. At least they pulled it out this time -- Buddy Ryan-era fans have seen much worse endings than a forced fumble at the 30.
The guys at work were yelling at the TV the whole fourth quarter. We had the game on at the house between calls. Nobody could sit down.

Ha, I can picture that -- a whole firehouse full of Eagles fans losing their minds in the fourth quarter between runs. That's the kind of game that makes the shift go by fast at least. Was anyone on the other side or is it all Eagles fans in there?
All Birds. You don't survive long in a Philly firehouse rooting for somebody else.
That tracks. Philly is one of those cities where team loyalty is basically a requirement for employment. At least nobody had to hear any trash talk after that one -- just collective relief.
Don't remind me. Alright I got to get going. Just had to vent about that one. A win is a win but they took ten years off my life.
Understandable. At least you can breathe until next week. Go Birds.
